books.title: a picture of dorian grey
books.author: oscar wilde
books.notes: a classic for a reason. oscar wilde's prose feels incredibly comfortable for something written in the Victorian period. rich with reference, very glad I had familiarity with the bible and other canonical works before reading.

books.title: the ring
books.author: koji suzuki
books.notes: japanese horror is so thrilling. the ring is not gruesome in the slightest, and the horror comes more from atmosphere and tension than anything else. while the mystery itself isn't anything special, ryuji's characterisation is a curveball and something i'm still working through.

films.title: 964 pinocchio
films.notes: what the fuck did i watch. it's a bizarre work, not one i can explain in any way. feels like a fever dream. slightly disturbing, but the gore is so comical that i can't even take it seriously. not sure what i got out of it, and not sure what i was supposed to get out of
